meta.description: Windows 11 Notepad (Tabbed UWP). Multi-document app — multiple tabs per window. Use NEWDOC to open a new tab instead of launching a second process. Use SENDKEYS for typing, clipboard operations (Ctrl+A, Ctrl+C, Ctrl+V, Ctrl+Z). Use READ to read the full document text. Use QUERYTREE to inspect top-level structure (limited AutomationId exposure).

Multi-document tabbed Notepad (Windows 11 UWP). One process, many tabs. Main interaction: READ → entire document text as a string SENDKEYS → type characters or keyboard shortcuts NEWDOC → new tab (returns HANDLE: of new or existing window) QUERYTREE → structural info only (TitleBar, Pane children); no text-edit AutomationId Key SENDKEYS shortcuts: {CTRL+A} select all {CTRL+C} copy {CTRL+V} paste {CTRL+Z} undo {CTRL+S} save {CTRL+W} close tab {CTRL+N} new tab (same as NEWDOC) {CTRL+P} print {ENTER} newline {BACK} backspace {DELETE} delete Targeting: use HANDLE:<hwnd> for precise tab targeting after NEWDOC. Window title pattern: "<filename> – Poznámkový blok" (Czech) or "<filename> – Notepad" (English); "Bez názvu" / "Untitled" = unsaved. To read clipboard: SENDKEYS {CTRL+A}{CTRL+C} then READ is unreliable for large text; prefer READ directly on the window for full content.

Control.label: Title bar — shows current document name and unsaved-changes indicator (*)
Name property = window title, e.g. "Untitled – Notepad" or "* myfile.txt – Notepad". The leading asterisk (*) indicates unsaved changes. Not directly actionable for text editing.

Control.note: synthetic — target is the hwnd, not an AutomationId
Control.label: The Notepad window / document area. READ returns full text content.

Target: use window title or HANDLE:<hwnd>. READ → multi-line string with full document text. SENDKEYS → types at current cursor position. To append to end: SENDKEYS {CTRL+END} first to move cursor to end. To select all: SENDKEYS {CTRL+A}. To replace all: SENDKEYS {CTRL+A} then type new text (replaces selection).
